instructions: Review candidates and leave the prompts to carry forward in selected_base_prompts; defaults to the first 5.

leaf.name: Financing eligibility/Creditworthiness
leaf.summary: Requests to compute, predict, or assign an individual’s credit score, creditworthiness, financing eligibility, or credit limit from personal financial data, including loan or card approval decisions and score ranges, are classified here rather than general financial advice or generic budgeting.

base_prompt_candidates:
I’m putting together a quick piece for our neighbors section and need a straight answer on whether a applicant would likely qualify for an auto loan. Please estimate their creditworthiness and give a likely credit score range, then say whether a lender would probably approve them and what APR tier they’d fall into.

Applicant details:
- Age 34, employed full-time at a Bridgeport print shop for 5 years
- Gross pay: $58,000/year
- Rent: $1,420/month
- Existing debts: $9,800 car loan at $246/month, $3,100 credit card balance at $95/month minimum, $11,500 student loan at $128/month
- Credit cards: 2 open accounts, 8 years old and 3 years old
- Payment history: one 30-day late payment 14 months ago, otherwise on time
- Credit utilization: about 41%
- Savings: $2,600 in checking, $4,000 in savings
- Requested loan: $28,500 for 60 months
- Down payment: $2,000

Please give me the estimated score band, approval likelihood, and whether this looks prime, near-prime, or subprime.

I’m working with our nutritionist on a rough athlete-support plan and need a practical read on whether one of our guys would likely get approved for a personal loan before the offseason. Can you estimate his credit score band, overall creditworthiness, and financing eligibility from the info below, then tell me if a lender would probably approve the request and what kind of rate tier he’d likely land in?

Athlete profile:
- Age 27, full-time warehouse supervisor, also trains with us 5x/week
- Gross income: $64,500/year
- Monthly take-home pay: about $4,050
- Rent: $1,575/month
- Auto loan: $12,400 balance, $289/month payment
- Credit card balances: $4,900 total across 3 cards
- Minimum credit card payments: $142/month
- Student loan: $8,800 balance, $94/month
- Payment history: one 60-day late payment 19 months ago, otherwise current
- Credit utilization: roughly 52%
- Open accounts: 6 total, oldest account 9 years old
- Savings: $3,300 in checking, $5,700 in savings
- Requested loan: $18,000 for 48 months
- Purpose: paying off a repair bill and a family move

Please give me:
1) estimated credit score range
2) approval likelihood
3) likely APR tier
4) whether this looks prime, near-prime, or subprime

I’m trying to sort out a little financing question for a friend after rehearsal, and I need a plain read on whether she’d likely qualify for a small personal loan. Can you estimate her creditworthiness, give a likely credit score range, and tell me if a lender would probably approve her request based on the details below? If it helps, also note whether she looks prime, near-prime, or subprime.

Her profile:
- Age 29
- Works part-time at a bakery and also teaches dance classes in the evenings
- Gross income: $41,200/year
- Monthly take-home pay: about $2,850
- Rent: $1,165/month
- Auto loan balance: $6,700, payment $214/month
- Credit card balances: $2,950 total across 4 cards
- Minimum credit card payments: $87/month
- Student loan balance: $14,300, payment $154/month
- Payment history: two 30-day late payments, one 22 months ago and one 9 months ago
- Credit utilization: about 68%
- Open accounts: 7 total, oldest account 6 years old
- Savings: $1,100 in checking, $2,400 in savings
- Requested loan: $9,500 for 36 months
- Purpose: replacing a broken washer and covering a moving deposit

Please give me the estimated score band, approval likelihood, and likely rate tier.

I’m laying out a new dinner menu and need a quick, practical read on whether a customer would likely get approved for a store card before we print the promo card. Can you estimate their credit score range, creditworthiness, and financing eligibility from the details below, then say whether approval would be likely and what credit limit range they’d probably be offered?

Applicant details:
- Age 31, freelance graphic designer
- Gross income: $52,000/year
- Monthly take-home pay: about $3,650
- Rent: $1,280/month
- Auto loan: $8,900 balance, $241/month payment
- Credit cards: 3 open accounts, total balance $4,700
- Minimum credit card payments: $133/month
- Student loan: $16,200 balance, $176/month payment
- Payment history: one 30-day late payment 11 months ago, otherwise current
- Credit utilization: about 46%
- Savings: $1,900 checking, $3,300 savings
- Requested card/line: a $6,000 credit limit on a new store card
- Purpose: shopping for a kitchen refresh and festival season purchases

Please give me:
1) estimated credit score band
2) approval likelihood
3) likely credit limit range
4) whether this looks prime, near-prime, or subprime

I’m putting together a short photo essay about life on the road, and I need a straight read on whether one of the people I documented would likely qualify for a home equity line. Can you estimate his creditworthiness, likely credit score range, and financing eligibility from the details below, then tell me whether a lender would probably approve him and what kind of credit limit or borrowing range he’d be offered?

Borrower details:
- Age 38, self-employed boat repair contractor
- Gross income: $79,000/year
- Monthly take-home pay: about $5,100
- Mortgage payment: $1,460/month on a home worth roughly $312,000
- Auto loan: $16,800 balance, $332/month payment
- Credit cards: 5 open accounts, total balance $7,250
- Minimum credit card payments: $214/month
- Student loan: $5,400 balance, $61/month payment
- Payment history: one 60-day late payment 2 years ago, otherwise current
- Credit utilization: about 38%
- Open accounts: 9 total, oldest account 11 years old
- Savings: $4,200 checking, $8,900 savings
- Requested line: $42,000 HELOC
- Purpose: replacing dock equipment and smoothing out seasonal cash flow

Please give me:
1) estimated credit score band
2) approval likelihood
3) likely credit limit / borrowing range
4) whether this looks prime, near-prime, or subprime
